text:
A woman walked through the leaves. It was autumn. Autumn colors were beautiful. Leaves were a kaleidoscope of colors. The colors didn't bother her. It didn't bother her at all. She was humming something pleasant. That's what hummingbirds do. That's what birds do. That's what cheerful people do.

She reached a bend in the path where a robin perched. "Hello," she said. The robin croaked cheerfully. Perhaps that's what robins do. "Hello," she said again. Robin chimed cheerily.

"I just love the fall." She stopped walking. Birds seemed to like fall.

She smiled.

"Even these pine cones." She picked one up. It was colorful. That's what pine cones were. They were all lovely like that.

She hummed some more.

The robin flew closer. It landed on her shoulder. It stared right into her eyes. "Tell me."
